column_description: unique Numeric identifier of the crime report.
Case number assigned to the reported crime.
value_description:
commonsense evidence: 

There is usually one case number for any reported crime except in the case of multiple homicide where a single report will produce one case number for each victim

if case_number appears > 1, it means this is a multiple homicide

column_description: Date of the occurrence of the incident being reported
A redacted address indicating in which city block the incident occurred
Illinois Uniform Crime Reporting code: a four digit code used to classify criminal incidents when taking reports.
A description of the kind of area where incident occurred
A true/false value indicating if an arrest has been made
A true/false value indicating if the incident is as case of domestic violence
A number indicating the geographic subdivision of the police district where the incident occurred
A two digit number identifying the legislative district (ward) where the incident occurred.
A number identifying one of 77 geographic areas where the incident occurred
A code identifying the type of crime reported according to the classification scheme used by the FBI.
The latitude where incident is reported to have occurred.
The longitude where incident is reported to have occurred.
value_description:
commonsense evidence: 

The precise location / coordinate: combines the longitude and latitude for plotting purposes. (latitude, longitude)
